DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-19-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHAFIQ MIRAHMADI'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT TO A NEUROPSYCHIATRIC EVALUATION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO MAY 28, 2021 AT 9:00 A.M. AT THE LAST HEARING THE COURT REQUESTED THAT THE PARTIES MEET AND CONFER ON THE SCOPE OF THE IME'S. IT DOESN'T APPEAR THAT THERE WAS ANY MEET AND CONFER FROM THE PLAINTIFF SIDE AFTER THE DECLARATIONS WERE SERVED. FURTHERMORE PLAINTIFF'S PAPERS WERE LATE AND WAS JUST ANOTHER REHASH OF PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENTS FROM THE FIRST HEARING WITH NOTHING HELPFUL AS TO THE SCOPE FOR THE IME'S. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AND COME UP WITH A PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE IME'S WHERE YOU CANNOT AGREE THEN PUT BOTH VERSIONS IN THE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiff Records From Lingen, Psyd
Off Calendar Jun-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jessica Dewitt, Esq. BRADY LAW GROUP, (415) 459-7300 for Plaintiff.
Anne C. Gritzer, Esq., BASSI, EDLIN, HUIE & BLUM LLP, (415) 397-9006 for Attorneys for Defendant, SHAFIQ MIRAHMADI
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600Court reporter,

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHAFIQ MIRAHMADI'S Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiff Records From Dr. Lingen.

Defendant Shafiq Mirahmadi's motion to compel production of records from Dr. Lingen is granted. The judge pro tem's report and recommendations are adopted. Plaintiff's interrogatory responses indicate Lingen was both a treating physician and an expert. As the former, Lingen's records are discoverable. No sanctions are awarded.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For Leave Of Court To Re-Open Deposition Of Plaintiff Jonathan Overstreet Villanueva
Off Calendar Jun-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jessica Dewitt, Esq. BRADY LAW GROUP, (415) 459-7300 for Plaintiff.
Anne C. Gritzer, Esq., BASSI, EDLIN, HUIE & BLUM LLP, (415) 397-9006 for Attorneys for Defendant, SHAFIQ MIRAHMADI
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600Court reporter,

Counsel for Defendant contests the Courts tenative ruling as to the 1.h hour time limit on plaintiff's deposition.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Notice Of Motion For Leave Of Court To Re-Open Deposition Of Plaintiff Jonathan Overstreet Villanueva

Defendant Shafiq Mirahmadi's motion to re-open plaintiff's deposition is granted. The judge pro tem's report and recommendations are adopted. Plaintiff has raised the issue of a traumatic brain injury not previously articulated. Defendants are granted an additional 1.5 hours of deposition - exclusive of breaks, objections and colloquy. No sanctions are awarded.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit To A Neuropsychiatric Evaluation
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Continued To May-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHAFIQ MIRAHMADI's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit To A Neuropsychiatric Evaluation.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The matter is continued to May 28, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. At the last hearing the court requested that the parties meet and confer on the scope of the IME's. It doesn't appear that there was any meet and confer from the plaintiff side after the declarations were served. Furthermore plaintiff's papers were late and was just another rehash of plaintiff's arguments from the first hearing with nothing helpful as to the scope for the IME's.

Parties to meet and confer and come up with a proposed order for the IME's where you cannot agree then put both versions in the proposed order.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
